name: DP Basics
description: Dynamic programming stores answers to repeated subproblems so they are solved once.
commonMistakes: Using DP when the subproblems do not overlap
Not defining the state clearly

name: Memoization
description: Top-down DP uses recursion plus a cache to avoid recomputation.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to store computed results
Using a memo table with the wrong dimensions

name: Tabulation
description: Bottom-up DP fills a table in dependency order without recursion.
exampleJava: for (int i = 2; i <= n; i++) dp[i] = dp[i - 1] + dp[i - 2];
commonMistakes: Filling states before their dependencies are ready
Misaligning array indices with the mathematical state

name: State Transition
description: Each DP state should be derived from smaller well-defined previous states.
commonMistakes: Skipping a valid choice in the recurrence
Mixing state meaning across indices

name: Space Optimization
description: When only recent states are needed, replace a full DP table with rolling variables or rows.
exampleJava: int prev2 = 0, prev1 = 1; int cur = prev1 + prev2;
commonMistakes: Overwriting a state before it is used
Optimizing space before understanding the original recurrence

name: Grid DP
description: Use row-column states when movement is constrained across a matrix or board.
commonMistakes: Ignoring blocked cells or boundaries
Using the wrong base row or base column initialization

name: Knapsack Style DP
description: Model take-or-skip choices over items and capacities or target sums.
commonMistakes: Updating 1D DP in the wrong direction
Using capacity states that go negative

name: Sequence DP
description: Track the best sequence ending at each position under an ordering rule.
commonMistakes: Allowing invalid sequence extensions
Forgetting to initialize each dp[i] to 1

name: String DP
description: Build DP over prefixes or suffixes of strings to compare, align, or match characters.
commonMistakes: Confusing indices of the string with indices of the dp table
Forgetting the extra 0th row and column

name: Interval DP
description: Solve subproblems on contiguous ranges and combine splits or inward moves.
exampleJava: dp[i][j] = Math.min(dp[i][j], cost(i, k, j) + dp[i][k] + dp[k + 1][j]);
commonMistakes: Iterating intervals in the wrong order
Not choosing a correct split variable
